# Let's load the quranic_verses.json and six_hadith_books.json to inspect.
import json, os, sys, re, unicodedata
qfile = "/mnt/data/file-HHumqWkCbpLvx5yFcuNaEH-quranic_verses.json"
hfile = "/mnt/data/file-GTpsjEG93EG6BAuDqWqgAJ-six_hadith_books.json"
print("files exist?", os.path.exists(qfile), os.path.exists(hfile))
with open(qfile, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    qdata = json.load(f)
with open(hfile, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    hdata = json.load(f)
print("Loaded quranic verses:", len(qdata))
print("Sample first item keys:", list(qdata[0].keys())[:20])
print("Loaded hadith items:", len(hdata))
print("Sample hadith keys (first item):", list(hdata[0].keys())[:20])

# Define normalization functions and search utilities
import re, unicodedata
arabic_diacritics = re.compile("""[\u0610-\u061A\u064B-\u065F\u0670\u06D6-\u06ED]""")
tatweel = '\u0640'

def normalize_arabic(s):
    if s is None:
        return ''
    # remove diacritics
    s = re.sub(arabic_diacritics, '', s)
    # remove tatweel
    s = s.replace(tatweel, '')
    # unify hamza forms: map أ إ آ -> ا ; ؤ ئ -> ء
    s = s.replace('أ','ا').replace('إ','ا').replace('آ','ا')
    s = s.replace('ؤ','ء').replace('ئ','ء')
    # normalize alef maqsura to ي? often map to ى -> ى??? Typically map ى to ي
    s = s.replace('ى','ي')
    # remove punctuation: Arabic and ASCII
    s = re.sub(r'[^ء-ي0-9A-Za-z\s]', ' ', s)  # keep arabic letters and numbers and ascii letters and spaces
    # collapse whitespace
    s = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', s).strip()
    return s.lower()

# Prepare normalized ayahs in a list
for i in range(5):
    print(qdata[i]['ayah_text'])
norms = [normalize_arabic(item['ayah_text']) for item in qdata]
print("Normalized sample:", norms[:5])

snippet = "لِكُلٍّ جَعَلْنَا مِنْكُمْ شِرْعَةً وَمِنْهَاجًا"
full_span = snippet  # same in this case
n_snip = normalize_arabic(snippet)
n_snip, snippet

# Compute similarity metrics between candidate and snippet
from difflib import SequenceMatcher

candidate = qdata[matches[0][0]]['ayah_text']
n_candidate = normalize_arabic(candidate)
n_snip = normalize_arabic(snippet)

# Longest common substring ratio (in characters) relative to snippet length
def longest_common_substring(a, b):
    # returns length of LCSUBSTR
    # dynamic programming O(n*m)
    la = len(a)
    lb = len(b)
    if la==0 or lb==0:
        return 0
    dp = [[0]*(lb+1) for _ in range(la+1)]
    best = 0
    for i in range(la-1, -1, -1):
        for j in range(lb-1, -1, -1):
            if a[i] == b[j]:
                dp[i][j] = dp[i+1][j+1] + 1
                if dp[i][j] > best:
                    best = dp[i][j]
    return best

lcs_len = longest_common_substring(n_candidate, n_snip)
ratio_lcs = lcs_len / max(len(n_snip),1)
# Token F1
tok_cand = n_candidate.split()
tok_snip = n_snip.split()
common = set(tok_cand).intersection(tok_snip)
precision = len(common)/len(tok_cand) if tok_cand else 0
recall = len(common)/len(tok_snip) if tok_snip else 0
f1 = 2*precision*recall/(precision+recall) if (precision+recall)>0 else 0
# SequenceMatcher ratio (character-level)
seq_ratio = SequenceMatcher(None, n_candidate, n_snip).ratio()

n_candidate, n_snip, lcs_len, ratio_lcs, f1, seq_ratio, candidate[:200]

content.text: {"correction":"وَأَنْزَلْنَا إِلَيْكَ الْكِتَابَ بِالْحَقِّ مُصَدِّقًا لِمَا بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ مِنَ الْكِتَابِ وَمُهَيْمِنًا عَلَيْهِ ۖ فَاحْكُمْ بَيْنَهُمْ بِمَا أَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ ۖ وَلَا تَتَّبِعْ أَهْوَاءَهُمْ عَمَّا جَاءَكَ مِنَ الْحَقِّ ۚ لِكُلٍّ جَعَلْنَا مِنْكُمْ شِرْعَةً وَمِنْهَاجًا ۚ وَلَوْ شَاءَ اللَّهُ لَجَعَلَكُمْ أُمَّةً وَاحِدَةً وَلَـٰكِنْ لِيَبْلُوَكُمْ فِي مَا آتَاكُمْ ۖ فَاسْتَبِقُوا الْخَيْرَاتِ ۚ إِلَى اللَّهِ مَرْجِعُكُمْ جَمِيعًا فَيُنَبِّئُكُمْ بِمَا كُنْتُمْ فِيهِ تَخْتَلِفُونَ"}
